html:
A Carrot is a common Vegetable. It can most often be found growing naturally in the ground, but these won't respawn after being picked. Carrots can also be Farmed by planting Seeds for a 31.6% chance of growing or using Carrot Seeds (Oblong Seeds), and they can be Cooked on a fire for extra health when eaten (see below) or used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes.

Carrots have a few miscellaneous interactions and uses. Carrots can be given to Bunnymen to befriend them, and underground they can also be obtained by deconstructing Rabbit Hutches with a Hammer or Deconstruction Staff, yielding five Carrots per Rabbit Hutch (Ten with the staff). Feeding a Carrot to a Bird in a Birdcage will produce 1–2 Carrot Seeds, as well as a 50% chance for 1 regular Seed. In Don't Starve Together, you will always get 1 Carrot Seed. Carrots will also lure Rabbits towards them, whether as bait for Traps, or simply dropped. This can be useful for getting Morsels, as the Rabbits will not run away when eating a Carrot dropped on the ground. However, if they are not taken care of, they will consume the entire carrot. Rabbits will not eat Carrots if they are left planted in the ground.

Roasted Carrot

A Roasted Carrot is a Vegetable Food item made by Cooking a Carrot on a Campfire, Fire Pit, or Star. It restores slightly more Health than its raw counterpart. It can be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes.

Usage

×10

×4

×4

Tips

  • Since planted Carrots never spoil, they can be used as an emergency food supply. And since they do not respawn, not picking them until it is necessary improves availability of food during times when food sources are otherwise scarce. However, it is worth considering during the second year that the Bearger can pick them.
  • Although Carrots can make good bait for many Rabbits, one must be careful, as Rabbits can overwhelm the traps and waste the Carrot.
  • Carrots do respawn in Spring. (DST only)
  • Players should collect a decent amount of carrots and leave some spare carrots, as it takes a long time to rot (10 days) as it will help with Hunger early on in the game.

Gallery

  • A carrot and 3 Traps around it - a great technique to catch many Rabbits at a time.
  • A Rabbit Hutch cluster, with several Carrots, Grass and a Pitchfork in the center.
  • Cooking a Carrot over a Dwarf Star.
  • Original HD Carrot icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Cooked Carrot icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.

“Yuck. I pulled this vegetable out of the dirt.”
–Wilson, when examining a Carrot.
“Yuck. It's all vegetabley.”
–Willow, when examining a Carrot.
“Is food. I guess.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Carrot.
“It's a carrot.”
–Wendy, when examining a Carrot.
“NUTRITIOUS”
–WX-78, when examining a Carrot.
“Daucus carota. Edible, and delicious.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Carrot.
“That's rabbit food.”
–Woodie, when examining a Carrot.
“I'm not a fan of vegetables.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Carrot.
“Looks nutritious. But I cannot eat it like this.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Carrot.
“Where's the prötein?”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Carrot.
“I sort of miss being forced to eat these.”
–Webber, when examining a Carrot.
“They must've named these after oranges.”
–Walani, when examining a Carrot.
“Fresh picked produce!”
–Warly, when examining a Carrot.
“Fer me night vision in me eye.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Carrot.
“'TIS A CARROT”
–Wilba, when examining a Carrot.
“Thanks, dirt!”
–Wormwood, when examining a Carrot.
“Makes for an aerodynamic projectile.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Carrot.
“Free food from the ground.”
–Winona, when examining a Carrot.
“Non-imps like to eat things like this.”
–Wortox, when examining a Carrot.
“Tasty root!”
–Wurt, when examining a Carrot.
“I get kind of disappointed when it doesn't turn out to be a rat.”
–Walter, when examining a Carrot.
“From the ground, straight to my mouth.”
–Wanda, when examining a Carrot.
A Carrot is a common Vegetable. It can most often be found growing naturally in the ground, but these won't respawn after being picked. Carrots can also be Farmed by planting Seeds for a 31.6% chance of growing or using Carrot Seeds (Oblong Seeds), and they can be Cooked on a fire for extra health when eaten (see below) or used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes.
Carrots have a few miscellaneous interactions and uses. Carrots can be given to Bunnymen to befriend them, and underground they can also be obtained by deconstructing Rabbit Hutches with a Hammer or Deconstruction Staff, yielding five Carrots per Rabbit Hutch (Ten with the staff). Feeding a Carrot to a Bird in a Birdcage will produce 1–2 Carrot Seeds, as well as a 50% chance for 1 regular Seed. In Don't Starve Together, you will always get 1 Carrot Seed. Carrots will also lure Rabbits towards them, whether as bait for Traps, or simply dropped. This can be useful for getting Morsels, as the Rabbits will not run away when eating a Carrot dropped on the ground. However, if they are not taken care of, they will consume the entire carrot. Rabbits will not eat Carrots if they are left planted in the ground.
Roasted Carrot[edit | edit source]
“Mushy.”
–Wilson, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Still vegetabley, but better for having been in fire.”
–Willow, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Is not more like meat after do cooking.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“It's a carrot that's gone through a gauntlet of fire.”
–Wendy, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“IT IS FLOPPY NOW”
–WX-78, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Daucus carota, roasted to perfection.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“That's cooked rabbit food.”
–Woodie, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“A little more refined, at least.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“My stomach should be able to process this in its current form.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Sad cööked carröts.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Easier for us both.”
–Webber, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Smells good!”
–Walani, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Could use a dash of olive oil and cilantro...”
–Warly, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Nice 'n soft fer me no teeth.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“'TIS A FWOOSH'D CARROT”
–Wilba, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Tiny circles for belly.”
–Wormwood, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“It's already cut up into tiny little projectiles for me.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Easier on the gums. Not that that matters.”
–Winona, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“I think non-imps like it better cooked.”
–Wortox, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“It was fine way it was.”
–Wurt, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“Yup. It's a cooked carrot.”
–Walter, when examining a Roasted Carrot.
“I didn't bother seasoning it.”
–Wanda, when examining a Roasted Carrot.

A carrot and 3 Traps around it - a great technique to catch many Rabbits at a time.
A Rabbit Hutch cluster, with several Carrots, Grass and a Pitchfork in the center.
Cooking a Carrot over a Dwarf Star.

And since they do not respawn, not picking them until it is necessary improves availability of food during times when food sources are otherwise scarce. However, it is worth considering during the second year that the Bearger can pick them. * Although Carrots can make good bait for many Rabbits, one must be careful, as Rabbits can overwhelm the traps and waste the Carrot. * Carrots do respawn in Spring.
